 * Solves the problem in O(N) time complexity.
 * It checks the longest balanced substring for the original string (no swap),
 * and then checks for substrings that can become balanced by flipping one character
 * using a swap with an external character (imbalance correction of +/- 2).
 * 
 * @param {string} s The binary string.
 * @returns {number} The maximum length of a balanced substring.
 */
function solve(s) {
    const N = s.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;

    // P[k] = prefix sum up to index k-1. Value: 1 for '1', -1 for '0'.

    // C0[k] = count of '0's in s[0...k-1]

        const currentP = P[k];

        if (first_occurrence.has(currentP)) {
